Settings_PlayErrorSoundDesc: Play a sound when a playback error occurs.

Settings_VolumeCurve_Desc: How volume slider maps to actual loudness
Settings_VolumeBoost_Tooltip: Allow volume above 100%. When disabled, higher max volume only increases precision for fine-tuning.
Settings_AudioNormalization_Tooltip: Automatically adjusts volume levels so all tracks play at a consistent loudness. No more reaching for the volume knob between songs.
Settings_NetProfileDesc: Adjusts buffer size and quality based on connection.
Settings_ClientDesc: Change if tracks fail to load (403 Error).
Settings_RamCacheDesc: Max number of images in memory. Lower = less RAM, more disk reads.
Settings_AutoSaveDownloadsDesc: Automatically save fully cached tracks to Downloads folder. Disabled = tracks stay in cache only (saves disk space).
Settings_ClearDownloadsConfirm: Delete all downloaded tracks? This cannot be undone.
Settings_SkipNTokenDesc: Automatically skip tracks requiring complex YouTube signature decryption (n-token). This speeds up playback, but some tracks will be unavailable.
Settings_NormalizationTarget_Desc: Target loudness in LUFS. Lower = quieter, higher = louder. -14 is streaming standard.
Settings_NormalizationMaxGain_Desc: Maximum amplification for quiet sections. Lower values prevent pumping artifacts.
Settings_GpuTextureCacheDesc: Skia texture cache limit for album art on GPU. Higher = smoother scrolling, but more RAM usage. Requires restart.
Settings_AutoMemoryCleanupDesc: Periodically free image caches, unused ViewModels and Skia buffers.
Settings_MemoryCleanupIntervalDesc: How often to perform automatic memory cleanup.
Settings_MemoryPressureThresholdDesc: When exceeded, aggressive cleanup is triggered. 0 = disabled.
Settings_ManualCleanupDesc: Immediately free caches and run garbage collector.
Settings_NormalizationTarget_Note: The Target Loudness and Max Gain are used by the built-in EBU R128 analyzer for tracks without metadata. When a track has YouTube loudness metadata, the gain is computed from that metadata directly — these sliders do not override it.
Settings_NormalizationDisable_Message: Without normalization you'll need to manually adjust the volume for each track — some songs are much louder or quieter than others. Are you sure you want to disable it?
Settings_NormalizationInfo_Body:
Audio normalization ensures every track plays at a similar loudness level, so you don't have to adjust the volume between songs.

🔍 How the loudness is determined
When a track comes from YouTube, the player uses loudness metadata that YouTube has already measured for every video. This means gain is applied instantly — no delay, no scanning.

For tracks without metadata (local files, or if YouTube data is missing), the player performs its own EBU R128 analysis. For local files this pre-scan takes about 50–150 ms. For streams it runs in real-time during the first 3 seconds.

Once measured, the gain value is saved in the database. Next time you play the same track, normalization is instant.

🎚️ Normalization Modes
• Bidirectional (Spotify style): both loud and quiet tracks are adjusted to the target level. Every song plays at the same perceived volume.
• Downward Only (YouTube style): only loud tracks are turned down. Quiet tracks are left as-is — just like on YouTube.

⚙️ Target Loudness (LUFS)
The reference level the algorithm aims for. The industry standard is −14 LUFS (used by Spotify and YouTube). Lower values (e.g. −20) make everything quieter, higher values (e.g. −8) make it louder.

Note: this setting only affects the built-in analyzer. Tracks with YouTube metadata use the gain derived from that metadata directly.

📈 Max Gain
Limits how much a quiet track can be amplified. A value of 3.0× means a track won't be boosted more than 3 times its original level, even if the algorithm says it should be. This prevents amplifying noise in very quiet recordings.
Audio_GainDesc: Adjusts playback volume to be consistent.

Recommendation_DpiBlocked: This is usually caused by DPI blocking/throttling. Please run a DPI bypass tool (such as zapret, GoodbyeDPI) or enable a VPN/proxy.
Recommendation_Copyright: This content is unavailable due to legal restrictions.
Recommendation_BotDetection: Wait a few minutes or change your IP address (VPN).

Extension_Install_SubTitle: Click the button below — the extension will download and prepare automatically.
Extension_Install_DownloadError: Failed to download the extension. Please check your network connection.
Extension_Path_Copied_Toast: Path copied! Paste it in the file browser (Ctrl+V) and select the folder.
Extension_Link_Copied_Toast: Address copied! Paste it in the browser address bar (Ctrl+V) and press Enter.
Extension_Guide_Chrome_Step1: Click the button below — the extensions page address will be copied. Paste it into the Chrome address bar (Ctrl+V) and press Enter.
Extension_Guide_Chrome_Step2: On the page that opens, find the «Developer mode» toggle in the top-right corner and turn it on.
Extension_Guide_Chrome_Step3: Click the «Load unpacked» button that appears in the top-left corner.
Extension_Guide_Chrome_Step4: A folder selection dialog opens. Press Ctrl+V (path is already copied via the button below), then press Enter or click «Select Folder».
Extension_Guide_Chrome_Step5: Find the «LMP Auth» card in the extensions list and click the «Details» button.
Extension_Guide_Chrome_Step6: Find the «Allow in Incognito» option and enable the toggle next to it.
Extension_Guide_Chrome_Step7: Done! Now go to Login Instructions — click here ↑.
Extension_Guide_Edge_Step1: Click the button below — the extensions page address will be copied. Paste it into the Edge address bar (Ctrl+V) and press Enter.
Extension_Guide_Edge_Step2: On the page that opens, find the «Developer mode» toggle at the bottom of the left sidebar and turn it on.
Extension_Guide_Edge_Step3: Click the «Load unpacked» button that appears on the top control bar.
Extension_Guide_Edge_Step4: A folder selection dialog opens. Press Ctrl+V (path is already copied via the button below), then press Enter or click «Select Folder».
Extension_Guide_Edge_Step5: Find the «LMP Auth» card in the extensions list and click the «Details» button.
Extension_Guide_Edge_Step6: Find the «Allow in InPrivate» option and enable the toggle next to it.
Extension_Guide_Edge_Step7: Done! Now go to Login Instructions — click here ↑.
Extension_Guide_Firefox_Step1: Click the button below — the debugging page address will be copied. Paste it into the Firefox address bar (Ctrl+V) and press Enter.
Extension_Guide_Firefox_Step2: On the page that opens, click «This Firefox» in the left sidebar.
Extension_Guide_Firefox_Step3: Click «Load Temporary Add-on...». In the dialog that opens, press Ctrl+V (path is copied via the button below), press Enter, then select any file in the extension folder (e.g. manifest.json).
Extension_Guide_Firefox_Step4: Click the button below — the add-ons page address will be copied. Paste it into the Firefox address bar (Ctrl+V) and press Enter.
Extension_Guide_Firefox_Step5: Find «LMP Auth» in the list and click on it. In the details that open, find «Run in Private Windows» and set it to «Allow».
Extension_Guide_Firefox_Step6: Done! Now go to Login Instructions — click here ↑.
Extension_Guide_Firefox_Warning: ⚠ Temporary Firefox extensions are removed on browser restart. For permanent use, Chrome, Edge, or Opera is recommended.
Extension_Guide_Opera_Step1: Click the button below — the extensions page address will be copied. Paste it into the browser address bar (Ctrl+V) and press Enter.
Extension_Guide_Opera_Step2: On the page that opens, find the «Developer mode» toggle and turn it on.
Extension_Guide_Opera_Step4: A folder selection dialog opens. Press Ctrl+V (path is already copied via the button below), then press Enter or click «Select Folder».
Extension_Guide_Opera_Step5: Find the «LMP Auth» card in the extensions list and open its details.
Extension_Guide_Opera_Step6: Find the «Allow in Incognito» option and enable the toggle next to it.
Extension_Guide_Opera_Step7: Done! Now go to Login Instructions — click here ↑.
Auth_Step_Pending: Waiting — complete the previous step first.
Auth_Incognito_Warning: Google actively rotates session keys in the background. To keep your session alive for months, strictly follow these freeze steps:
Auth_Incognito_Step1: Press Ctrl+Shift+N — a new Incognito (Private) window will open.
Auth_Incognito_Step2: In the Incognito window, click the button below — the YouTube address will be copied. Paste it in the address bar (Ctrl+V) and press Enter.
Auth_Incognito_Step3: Sign in to your Google account on the YouTube page that opens.
Auth_Incognito_Step4: After signing in, click the button below — the freeze address will be copied. Paste it into the same address bar (Ctrl+V) and press Enter. The page will freeze — that is expected.
Auth_Incognito_Step5: Click the extensions icon on the browser toolbar (puzzle 🧩 icon or «Extensions»), find «LMP Auth» and click on it.
Auth_Incognito_Step6: In the extension popup that opens, click the «Send to LMP» button.
Auth_Incognito_Step7: Return to the player — the authorization window will close automatically.
Auth_Incognito_Step5_Hint: If the window did not close and the field below is empty — open the extension popup again. If it says something like «copied» — just press Ctrl+V in the field below and click «Sign in». If a different error appeared — click «What do statuses mean?» in the extension popup.
Auth_Tooltip_ToProtocol: Click to go to Login Instructions
